body:
See https://github.com/link-assistant/model-in-browser, we need smalles model that will actually help us to formalization matching to wikipedia and so on, we should provide the model with options to choose from, and let it select best match. And that should be confirmed using unit tests.

Also may be we can take useful ideas from https://habr.com/ru/articles/1047344/ (but only if they are better, than what I proposed).

Also by default it is turned off and not loaded. It should be loaded only if user explicitly enables it in settings.

Only models that are actually fit on user's hardware must be displayed, so we don't overload user with options.

And they should be sorted by public ratings. Everything downloaded on demand only, nothing is downloaded in advance or included in application package or web UI in any place.

We still keep using neural networks as non goal, but we can try delegate some functions to LLMs later so we can make some hybrid later if needed, but at the moment we advertise our system as formal first (not 100% formal, but preferably formal), meaning LLMs are never in control - never at steering wheel.

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
title: We can add experimental fallback for formalization using small models
